description: Changes tensor shape without changing element values; explicit target shape reshape.
Element-wise type conversion to a new element type with the same dimensions.
Explicit HLO copy primitive; semantically duplicates an array and is internally decomposed into CopyStart/CopyDone in HLO.
TAIDL alias for element-wise natural exponential x -> e^x.
Composes one array from multiple operands by joining them along a chosen dimension.
Bit-level reinterpretation of storage into a new element type; shape may change to preserve total bytes.
Permutes tensor dimensions according to a specified permutation.
Extracts a bounded sub-array from the input tensor using start/limit/stride indices.
Dot product / matrix-vector / matrix-matrix multiplication over contracted dimensions.
Produces a tensor from a compile-time constant literal.
Adds dimensions or expands sizes by duplicating operand data across the new dimensions.
Element-wise maximum of two tensors with compatible shapes.
Element-wise minimum of two tensors with compatible shapes.
TAIDL helper that expands to a less-than compare followed by select between two choices.
web_source_note: Compare and Select sections; helper behavior inferred from TAIDL template expansion.

description: TAIDL helper that expands to scalar compare-to-zero tests, logical and, then select between two choices.
web_source_note: Compare and Select sections; helper behavior inferred from TAIDL template expansion.

description: Element-wise XOR of two tensors.
Element-wise addition of two tensors.
Produces the input tensor with a slice overwritten by an update tensor at dynamic start indices.
Element-wise subtraction of rhs from lhs.
Element-wise product of two tensors.
Element-wise division of lhs by rhs.
Applies a user-provided reduction computation over one or more dimensions.
Represents an argument input to a computation; present in HLO but not StableHLO.
TAIDL shorthand for a sum reduction; semantically equivalent to reduce using addition as the combiner.
web_source_note: Reduce section; add combiner is TAIDL aliasing/inference.

description: Element-wise natural exponential x -> e^x.
